requirements.full_sentence:
All loans eligible to be categorised as Priority Sector Lending under the
erstwhile Master Directions on PSL dated September 04, 2020 (updated from time to
time) shall continue to be eligible for such categorisation under these Directions, till
maturity.
requirements.object: for such categorisation under these Directions, till maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
1.1 These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Priority Sector
Lending - Targets and Classification) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Priority Sector Lending - Targets and Classification) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:

the earlier Directions on the subject, namely, the Reserve Bank of India (Priority
Sector Lending - Targets and Classification) Directions, 2020 (Ref.
requirements.object: April 01, 2025, and shall supersede the earlier Directions on the subject, namely, the Reserve Bank of India (Priority Sector Lend

requirements.full_sentence:
4.1 In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
<image>
requirements.object: to them below: <image>

requirements.full_sentence:
activities allied to agriculture shall include dairy, fisheries,
animal husbandry, poultry, bee-keeping, sericulture and similar activities.
requirements.object: husbandry, poultry, bee-keeping, sericulture and similar activities

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Non-Corporate Farmers (NCF) shall include individual farmers including Small
and Marginal Farmers1 (SMFs), proprietorship firms of farmers directly engaged
in agriculture and allied activities, and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) or Joint Liability
Groups (JLGs) i.e., group of individual farmers, provided banks maintain
disaggregated data of such loans.
requirements.object: Small and Marginal Farmers1 (SMFs), proprietorship firms of farmers directly engaged in agriculture and allied activities, and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) or Joint Liability Groups (JLGs) i.e., group of individual farmers

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) "On-lending" means loans sanctioned by banks to eligible intermediaries for
onward lending.
requirements.object: to eligible intermediaries for onward lending

requirements.full_sentence:
4.2 All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
has been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as has been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modi

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3 All loans categorised as Priority Sector Lending (PSL) under the erstwhile
Master Directions on PSL dated September 04, 2020 (updated as on June 21,
2024) shall continue to be eligible for such categorisation under these
Directions till maturity.
requirements.object: for such categorisation under these Directions till maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
6.1 For the purpose of priority sector lending, ANBC shall be computed as follows:
Bank Credit in India [as prescribed in item No.VI of Form `A' under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934] I
Bills rediscounted with RBI and other approved Financial Institutions II Net Bank Credit (NBC)* III(I-II) Outstanding Deposits under RIDF and other eligible funds with NABARD, NHB, SIDBI and MUDRA Ltd in lieu of non-achievement of priority sector lending targets/sub-targets + outstanding PSLCs
IV
Eligible amount for exemptions on issuance of long-term bonds for infrastructure and affordable housing as per [Reserve Bank of India (Resource Raising Norms) Directions, 2025 as applicable to Commercial Banks and Small Finance Banks]2
V
Advances extended in India against the incremental FCNR (B)/NRE deposits, qualifying for exemption from CRR/SLR requirements, as per the Reserve Bank's circulars DBOD.No.Ret.BC.36/12.01.001/2013-14 dated August 14, 2013 read with DBOD.No.Ret.BC.93/12.01.001/2013- 14 dated January 31, 2014, DBOD mailbox clarification issued on February 6, 2014 and UBD.BPD.(PCB).CIR.No.5/13.01.000/2013-14 dated August 27, 2013 read with UBD.BPD.(PCB).Cir.No.72/13.01.000/ 2013-14 dated June 11, 2014.3
VI
Investments made by public sector banks in the Recapitalization Bonds floated by Government of India
VII
Other investments eligible to be treated as priority sector (e.g.
requirements.object: Bank Credit in India [as prescribed in item No

requirements.full_sentence: Banks shall not deduct / net any amount like provisions, accrued interest, etc.
requirements.object: amount like provisions, accrued interest, etc

requirements.full_sentence:

Exposures (CEOBSE), banks shall be guided by the [Reserve Bank of India

Reserve Bank of India (Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions,
2025, as applicable to Small Finance Banks, Urban Co-operative Banks and
Regional Rural Banks.
requirements.object: [Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025, and Reserve Bank of India (Prudential Nor

calculation of credit risk exposure attached to off-balance sheet items, banks

Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025]4
requirements.object: of India (Local Area Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025]4

grandfathered loans, for computation of ANBC:
a.

conditions:
if banks subtract prudential write

requirements.full_sentence:
6.4 While calculating Net Bank Credit as above, if banks subtract prudential write
off at Corporate/Head Office level, it shall be ensured that the credit to priority
sector and all sub-sectors so written off shall also be subtracted category wise
from priority sector target and sub-target achievement.
requirements.object: credit to priority sector and all sub-sectors so written off shall also be subtracted category wise from priority sector target an

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments or any
other items which are treated as eligible for classification under priority sector
target/sub-target achievement, shall also form part of Adjusted Net Bank Credit.
requirements.object: Adjusted Net Bank Credit

requirements.full_sentence:
6.5 All banks shall adhere to the respective licencing and operating guidelines
issued by the Department of Regulation, RBI as updated from time to time.
requirements.object: licencing and operating guidelines issued by the Department of Regulation, RBI as updated from time to time

requirements.full_sentence: However, foreign banks with less than 20 branches have an option to reckon the CEOBSE extended to borrowers for eligible priority sector activities for achievement of priority sector target, subject to the condition that the CEOBSE (both priority sector and non-priority sector excluding interbank exposure) shall be added to the ANBC in the denominator for computation of PSL targets.
requirements.object: ANBC in the denominator for computation of PSL targets

requirements.full_sentence: However, lending to Medium Enterprises, Social Infrastructure and Renewable Energy shall be reckoned for priority sector achievement up to 15 per cent of ANBC only.
requirements.object: sector achievement up to 15 per cent of ANBC only

requirements.full_sentence:
7.2 The priority sector lending targets for UCBs shall be as follows:
Categories Targets as a percentage of ANBC or
CEOBSE, whichever is higher Total Priority Sector 60% Micro Enterprises 7.5% Advances to Weaker Sections 12%


8.

requirements.full_sentence:
With effect from FY 2024-25, a higher weight (125%) shall be
assigned to the incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where the
credit flow is comparatively lower (per capita PSL less than ₹9,000), and a lower
weight (90%) will be assigned for incremental priority sector credit in the identified
districts where the credit flow is comparatively higher (per capita PSL greater than
₹42,000).
requirements.object: incremental priority sector credit in the identified districts where the credit flow is comparatively lower (per capita PSL less th

requirements.full_sentence:
8.2 The banks shall continue to report the actual outstanding amount in Quarterly
Priority Sector Advances (QPSA) returns as hitherto.
requirements.object: actual outstanding amount in Quarterly Priority Sector Advances (QPSA) returns as hitherto

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: The provisions of para 9.5 shall not be applicable to RRBs, UCBs, SFBs
and LABs.
requirements.object: UCBs, SFBs and LABs

requirements.full_sentence:
9.3 Ancillary Services
The following shall be eligible to be classified in this category:
i.
requirements.object: classified in this category: i

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) The definition of MSMEs shall be as given in the FIDD.MSME &
NFS.12/06.02.31/2017-18 dated July 24, 2017 as updated from time to time.
requirements.object: the FIDD

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) All bank loans to MSMEs shall qualify for classification under priority sector
lending.
requirements.object: priority sector lending

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Loans up to ₹50 crore to Start-ups10, that conform to the definition of MSME,
shall also be eligible to be classified under this category.
requirements.object: be classified under this category

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Factoring transactions pertaining to MSMEs taking place through the Trade
Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) shall also be eligible for
classification under priority sector.
requirements.object: classification under priority sector

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Export credit to agriculture and MSMEs shall be eligible for classification as
PSL in the respective categories [and subject to the aggregate limits
mentioned therein.]13
requirements.object: as PSL in the respective categories [and subject to the aggregate limits mentioned therein

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Export Credit (other than that classified under agriculture and MSME) shall
be eligible for classification as priority sector lending as per the following
requirements.object: as priority sector lending as per the following

requirements.full_sentence:
Housing loans which are backed by long term bonds shall not be classified
under priority sector, as they are exempted from inclusion in ANBC.
requirements.object: sector, as they are exempted from inclusion in ANBC

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments made by UCBs in bonds issued by NHB/HUDCO on or after
April 1, 2007 shall not be eligible for classification under priority sector.
requirements.object: under priority sector

requirements.full_sentence:
Loans for repairs to damaged dwelling units shall be eligible for priority
sector classification subject to the following limits:

Centres with population of 10 lakh and above but below 50 lakh
12 57
Centres with population below 10 lakh
10 44

may be specified in their internal policy.]22

adherence to the prescribed cap by averaging the eligible portfolio under on-

Bank credit to registered NBFCs (other than MFIs) for on-lending will be eligible for
classification as priority sector lending under the respective categories subject to the
following conditions:

requirements.full_sentence:
PSL eligibility for COVID-19 measures
Outstanding loans extended under policy measures to mitigate the financial impact
of COVID-19, as detailed in Annex-IV, shall be eligible for classification as priority
sector lending.
requirements.object: as priority sector lending

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) The data on priority sector advances shall be furnished by banks at [quarterly
and annual]39 intervals as per the respective reporting format, within fifteen days
and one month, respectively from the end of each quarter and financial year.
requirements.object: at [quarterly and annual]39 intervals as per the respective reporting format, within fifteen days and one month, respectively fr

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) In respect of RRBs, the data on priority sector advances, in the above format,
shall be furnished to NABARD at quarterly and annual intervals.
requirements.object: at quarterly and annual intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) UCBs shall be guided by Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Filing of
Supervisory Returns) Directions - 2024 dated February 27, 2024, as updated from
time to time, as regards submission of data on priority sector advances.
requirements.object: Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions - 2024 dated February 27, 2024, as updated from time

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) All banks (excluding UCBs under all-inclusive directions) reporting shortfall in
priority sector lending vis-à-vis the prescribed target/sub-targets shall be
allocated amounts for contribution to the Rural Infrastructure Development
Fund (RIDF) and other funds with NABARD/NHB/SIDBI/MUDRA Ltd., as
decided by the Reserve Bank from time to time.
requirements.object: contribution to the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) and other funds with NABARD/NHB/SIDBI/MUDRA Ltd

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the terms and
conditions of the funds shall be as decided by Reserve Bank of India.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The interest rates payable to banks for their contribution to RIDF and other
funds shall be as follows:
S.

Common guidelines for Priority Sector Loans
Banks shall also comply with the following common guidelines for all categories of
priority sector advances.
requirements.object: following common guidelines for all categories of priority sector advances

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Rate of interest: The rates of interest charged on loans shall be in accordance
with the [Reserve Bank of India (Interest Rate on Advances) Directions, 2025, as
applicable to Commercial Banks, Small Finance Banks, Regional Rural Banks,
Local Area Banks and Urban Co-operative Banks.]40
40 Substituted vide Reserve Bank of India (Priority Sector Lending - Targets and Classification) (Amendment) Directions, 2026 dated January 19, 2026
<image>
requirements.object: the [Reserve Bank of India (Interest Rate on Advances) Directions, 2025, as applicable to Commercial Banks, Small Finance Banks,

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Service charges: No loan related [charges (including guarantee fees of credit
guarantee schemes),]41 and ad hoc service charges/inspection charges shall be
levied on priority sector loans up to ₹50,000.
requirements.object: sector loans up to ₹50,000

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Record of Receipt, Sanction/Rejection/Disbursement: Record shall be
maintained by the bank of the date of receipt, sanction, disbursement, rejection
with reasons thereof, etc.
requirements.object: bank of the date of receipt, sanction, disbursement, rejection with reasons thereof, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Acknowledgement of loan applications:
Banks shall provide
acknowledgement of receipt of applications for priority sector loans.
requirements.object: of applications for priority sector loans

requirements.full_sentence:
Bank Boards
shall prescribe the time limit within which the bank communicates its decision in
writing to the applicants.
requirements.object: within which the bank communicates its decision in writing to the applicants

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Banks shall ensure that loans categorised as priority sector lending are granted for
approved purposes and the end use is monitored, by putting in place proper
internal systems and controls.
requirements.object: as priority sector lending are granted for approved purposes and the end use is monitored, by putting in place proper in

requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) Each priority sector loan shall be classified only in any one of the eight identified
categories specified in para 5 of these Master Directions.
requirements.object: any one of the eight identified categories specified in para 5 of these Master Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
iv) Sellers/Buyers: Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs), Regional Rural Banks
(RRBs), Local Area Banks (LABs), Small Finance Banks (SFBs) and Urban Co-
operative Banks (UCBs) who have originated PSL eligible category loans subject
to such regulations as may be issued by the Bank.
requirements.object: Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the overall
<image>
target and sectoral targets for lending to agriculture and micro enterprises, banks
are required to achieve specified sub-target for lending to Small and Marginal
Farmers.
requirements.object: for lending to Small and Marginal Farmers

conditions:
if a bank
is having shortfall in achievement of the overall target only
requirements.full_sentence:
However, if a bank
is having shortfall in achievement of the overall target only, as applicable to it, may
buy any of the available PSLCs.
requirements.object: available PSLCs

requirements.full_sentence:
However, as on the
reporting date, the bank must have met the priority sector target by way of the sum
of outstanding priority sector lending portfolio and net of PSLCs issued and
purchased.
requirements.object: sector target by way of the sum of outstanding priority sector lending portfolio and net of PSLCs issued and purchased

requirements.full_sentence:
To the extent of shortfall in the achievement of target, banks may be
required to invest in RIDF/other funds as hitherto.
requirements.object: in RIDF/other funds as hitherto

requirements.full_sentence:
xiv) Disclosures: Both seller and buyer shall report the amount of PSLCs (category-
wise) sold and purchased during the year in the 'Disclosures to the Balance
Sheet'.
requirements.object: PSLCs (category- wise) sold and purchased during the year in the 'Disclosures to the Balance Sheet'

Priority sector
classification shall be available to outstanding credit extended under the measures
specified below:
requirements.object: credit extended under the measures specified below:

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) In terms of press release: 2021-2022/177 dated May 7, 2021, an on-tap
liquidity window of ₹50,000 crore with tenors of up to three years at the repo
rate till March 31, 2022 was opened to boost provision of immediate liquidity
for ramping up COVID-related healthcare infrastructure and services in the
country.
